scout_targets.field_a: Manganese neurotoxicology — Mn2+ accumulation in basal ganglia causing manganism, dopaminergic oxidative damage via Fenton-like ROS, mitochondrial Complex I inhibition
scout_targets.field_c: Deinococcus radiodurans Mn-antioxidant biology — Mn2+-phosphate-peptide (Mn-OP) complexes catalytically scavenge ROS, MnSOD-independent protein protection, radiation/oxidative stress survival
bridge_concepts: Mn2+-orthophosphate-peptide ternary complexes (Mn-OP) — catalytic SOD-mimetic antioxidants from D. radiodurans (Daly 2010, mBio 2022); synthetic DP1 decapeptide (DEHGTAVMLK)-Mn2+-Pi (PNAS 2024)
Mn2+/Mn3+ catalytic cycling — same redox chemistry causes ROS in neurons (free Mn2+) or scavenges ROS in bacteria (complexed Mn2+); speciation determines outcome
MnSOD-independent non-enzymatic antioxidant defense — supplants MnSOD in D. radiodurans and C. elegans; completely unstudied in mammalian neurons
Mitochondrial Complex I as convergence point — free Mn2+ inhibits Complex I (manganism) vs Mn-OP protects mitochondrial proteins (D. radiodurans)
DMT1/SLC11A2 transporter-determined subcellular Mn2+ speciation in neurons

scout_targets.field_a: Plant melatonin stress biology — SNAT/ASMT melatonin biosynthesis under heat/UV/drought stress, melatonin-AFMK-AMK ROS scavenging cascade, NPQ photoprotection enhancement, chloroplast-to-nucleus retrograde signaling
scout_targets.field_c: Coral bleaching / Symbiodiniaceae thermal tolerance — heat-induced ROS overproduction in dinoflagellate symbiont chloroplasts, PSII photoinhibition, symbiosis breakdown, molecular basis of thermal tolerance poorly understood
bridge_concepts: SNAT/ASMT melatonin biosynthesis pathway — characterized in plants; dinoflagellates confirmed to synthesize melatonin via identical tryptophan pathway (Balzer & Hardeland 1996)
Melatonin-AFMK-AMK ROS scavenging cascade — each metabolite more potent; characterized in plant chloroplasts under heat stress
NPQ-mediated PSII photoprotection by melatonin — enhances non-photochemical quenching under heat in plants
Chloroplast-to-nucleus retrograde signaling via melatonin in plants
Tryptophan hydroxylase circadian regulation in dinoflagellate Gonyaulax polyedra (Balzer & Hardeland 1996)

scout_targets.field_a: Volcanic glass dissolution kinetics (volcanology/geochemistry) — TST rate law, saturation index, PHREEQC speciation modeling, BET-normalized reactive surface area, passivation layer formation
scout_targets.field_c: Amorphous solid dispersion (ASD) dissolution (pharmaceutical science) — ~40% new drugs poorly soluble, ASD dissolution prediction is unsolved, empirical models, drug recrystallization during dissolution
bridge_concepts: TST dissolution rate law: Rate = k+*A*(1-exp(DGr/RT)) — never applied to pharmaceutical ASD dissolution
Saturation index SI = log(IAP/Ksp) replacing empirical sink conditions
BET-normalized reactive surface area for consistent dissolution rate normalization
Passivation layer kinetics (Gin 2015 Nature Materials) for polymer-rich gel layer prediction
Coupled dissolution-crystallization modeling for ASD supersaturation window prediction
PHREEQC-style speciation modeling for pH-dependent drug ionization

strategy_diversification.novel_strategy: all three — none used as primary in any prior session
